description: The Hells Angels Motorcycle Club is an international outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1948 in California. The article focuses on criminal allegations and incidents involving its New York chapters, which were established in 1969. Members are accused of drug trafficking, extortion, and violence, and have links with Mafia families and the Medellín Cartel.

country_links.evidence_quote: The club also distributes methamphetamine in the state and smuggles marijuana into the United States from Canada through the St. Regis Mohawk Reservation.
country_links.context: The Hells Angels smuggles marijuana into the United States from Canada through the St. Regis Mohawk Reservation.

country_links.evidence_quote: is responsible for coordinating all Hells Angels activities in the Eastern United States as well as those of chapters in Canada and Europe.
country_links.context: The Hells Angels coordinate activities of chapters in Europe.

country_links.evidence_quote: Kittel and Zito surrendered to the United States Embassy in Tokyo on July 22, 1985 after they were the subject of a nationwide dragnet by Japanese police at the request of the FBI.
country_links.context: Two Hells Angels members were arrested in Japan and later extradited to the United States.
